title: Madayi Kavu
tagline: Madayi Kavu — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.description: Thiruvarkadu Bhagavathi Temple (a.k.a. Madayi Kavu) is a famous temple dedicated to Bhadrakali in Kerala. The deity is the Fierce form of Bhadrakali. The Bhagavathy is addressed by tantrics in the vicinity as Tiruvarkkad Achchi due to this.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Eripuram
topPlaces.description: Eripuram is a village within the Payyanur Taluk of Kannur district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Aduthila
topPlaces.description: Aduthila is a village between Ezhome and Madayi Panchayath, in the Indian state of Kerala. Aduthila literally means the nearest home (in Malayalam aduth means near and illam means family or home).

topPlaces.name: Madai Vadukunda Shiva Temple
topPlaces.description: The Madai Vadukunda Shiva temple is a Hindu temple located in Madai (Kannur Taluk district of Kerala).It is believed to have been constructed by "Kolathiri" Kings during Middle Ages on a plateau land generally known now as "Madai Para". This is located 22 km (14 mi) north of Kannur, the Headquarters Town.

topPlaces.name: Madayi Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Madayi Mosque, also known as the Matayi Palli, and as the Pazhayangadi Mosque or as the Malik ibn Dinar Mosque, Madayi, is a Shāfiʿī Sunni mosque, located at Pazhayangadi in the Kannur district in northern Kerala, India. It is one of the oldest mosques in Kerala, with local legends dating the mosque from 518 AH (1124/1125 CE).

topPlaces.name: Pazhayangadi
topPlaces.description: Pazhayangadi (Also called Payangadi), is a small township, approximately 23 km (14 mi) north of district headquarters Kannur, 13.5 km (8.4 mi) south of Payyanur and 13.5 km (8.4 mi) west of Taliparamba in the Indian state of Kerala. In the local language of Malayalam it literally means "Old Market".

topPlaces.name: Tahir Mosque, Payangadi
topPlaces.description: The Tahir Mosque is an Ahmadiyya mosque located in Payangadi, in the Kannur district, in the state of Kerala, India. The foundation stone was laid in July 1919 by Hadhrat Maulana Gulam Rasool Rajeki sahib.

topPlaces.name: Kozhibazar
topPlaces.description: KozhiBazar is a small village approximately 23 kilometers (14 mi) north of Kannur town, in Kerala, South India.

topPlaces.name: Ezhome
topPlaces.description: Ezhome is a census town in Kannur district in the Indian state of Kerala.

items.note: Eripuram is a village within the Payyanur Taluk of Kannur district in the state of Kerala, India.
Aduthila is a village between Ezhome and Madayi Panchayath, in the Indian state of Kerala. Aduthila literally means the nearest home (in Malayalam aduth means near and illam means family or home).

The Madai Vadukunda Shiva temple is a Hindu temple located in Madai (Kannur Taluk district of Kerala).It is believed to have been constructed by "Kolathiri" Kings during Middle Ages on a plateau land generally known now as "Madai Para". This is located 22 km (14 mi) north of Kannur, the Headquarters Town.

The Madayi Mosque, also known as the Matayi Palli, and as the Pazhayangadi Mosque or as the Malik ibn Dinar Mosque, Madayi, is a Shāfiʿī Sunni mosque, located at Pazhayangadi in the Kannur district in northern Kerala, India. It is one of the oldest mosques in Kerala, with local legends dating the mosque from 518 AH (1124/1125 CE).

Pazhayangadi (Also called Payangadi), is a small township, approximately 23 km (14 mi) north of district headquarters Kannur, 13.5 km (8.4 mi) south of Payyanur and 13.5 km (8.4 mi) west of Taliparamba in the Indian state of Kerala. In the local language of Malayalam it literally means "Old Market".

The Tahir Mosque is an Ahmadiyya mosque located in Payangadi, in the Kannur district, in the state of Kerala, India. The foundation stone was laid in July 1919 by Hadhrat Maulana Gulam Rasool Rajeki sahib.
KozhiBazar is a small village approximately 23 kilometers (14 mi) north of Kannur town, in Kerala, South India.
Ezhome is a census town in Kannur district in the Indian state of Kerala.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Madayi Kavu?
faq.a: The best time to visit Madayi Kavu is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Madayi Kavu?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (111 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (103 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Madayi Kavu?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Madayi Kavu at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Madayi Kavu?
faq.a: Stays in Madayi Kavu start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Madayi Kavu?
faq.a: The top attractions are Eripuram, Aduthila, Madai Vadukunda Shiva Temple, Madayi Mosque, Pazhayangadi.
